Description: Animations that help users understand the structure of the application and how to move through it.

SubAnimations.Effect: Cross-fades, slides, or shared element transitions between views.
Elements smoothly resizing or moving to new positions when the layout changes.
Off-canvas menus sliding in or overlays springing into view.

Description: Immediate visual responses to user actions, confirming that an interaction has occurred.

SubAnimations.Effect: Subtle button presses, toggle switches, or heart icons bubbling up.
Spinners, progress bars, or skeleton screens indicating back-end activity.
Scale changes, shadow elevations, or color shifts on mouse-over.

Description: Animations that establish relationships between elements and guide focus.

SubAnimations.Effect: List items appearing one by one with a slight delay.
Pulsing borders or background glows on active form fields.
Content expanding and contracting vertically.

Description: Motion design used to create emotional connection and reinforce brand identity.

SubAnimations.Effect: Complex vector animations used for storytelling or hero sections.
Background elements moving slower than foreground elements on scroll.
Floating backgrounds, interactive 3D models, or mouse-trailing effects.

Description: Animations that solve specific technical display problems or optimize perception.

SubAnimations.Effect: Continuously scrolling text or logo ribbons.
Characters appearing as if typed in real-time.
Bouncy icons triggered by vertical over-scrolling on mobile.

Description: High-end motion effects that respond to the user's scroll position or spatial movement.

SubAnimations.Effect: Elements fading, scaling, or sliding in as they enter the viewport.
Vertical scrolling translates to horizontal movement of content blocks.
Layered backgrounds moving at different speeds while foreground content remains 'stuck'.
Elements following a complex SVG path as the user scrolls down.

Description: Complex vector-based animations that change shape or animate path strokes.

SubAnimations.Effect: One SVG shape smoothly transforming into another (e.g., Play button to Pause).
Simulating the drawing of icons or illustrations as if by hand.
Distortions like 'Gooey' effects or grainy noise applied to vector shapes.

Description: Animations used to represent changing data or system status changes.

SubAnimations.Effect: Bars, lines, or points smoothly transitioning when underlying data updates.
Numbers rapidly ticking up or down to reflect new values.
List items shuffling positions smoothly when filtered or sorted.

Description: Animations designed to simulate physical touch or create emotional comfort.

SubAnimations.Effect: An element pulling back slightly before moving forward, then bouncing at the end.
Subtle shimmering pulses that represent content structure before it loads.
Simulating a vibration or 'click' through subtle shaking or scale dips.

Description: Animations focus on digital type, moving beyond static letters to interactive storytelling.

SubAnimations.Effect: Morphing font weights, widths, or slants dynamically in response to scroll or hover.
Text sliding along complex organic or geometric curves.
Characters cycling through random symbols before settling on the correct word.

Description: Reimagining the cursor as an active part of the interface feedback loop.

SubAnimations.Effect: Buttons or icons being 'pulled' toward the cursor when it gets close.
Shadows, sparkles, or text following the cursor with smoothed physics.
The cursor acting as a 'flashlight' to reveal hidden content layers underneath.

Description: Motion governed by mathematical algorithms, noise, and complex physics simulations.

SubAnimations.Effect: Organic, flow-like movements that never repeat exactly.
Thousands of dots reacting to mouse movement or screen events.
Simulating high-fidelity liquid or smoke movement on the screen.

Description: Motion that utilizes the Z-axis to create a sense of three-dimensional space.

SubAnimations.Effect: Elements tilting and reflecting light based on mouse position.
The viewpoint moving through a 3D landscape of UI components.
Layers pushing back or coming forward to indicate hierarchy depth.
